# Game Concept: [Game Title]

## Elevator Pitch

[One sentence that describes the game. Example:]
> A pixel-art roguelike where you roll dice to move around a circular board, fighting monsters and building a village between runs.

## Core Fantasy

[What does the player imagine themselves doing? What emotion drives them?]

> The player imagines themself as a faith-wielding village chief, making risky gambles with dice rolls while managing the weight of their equipment and the burden of their beliefs. The fantasy is about meaningful risk — every roll matters, every item has weight, every choice shapes both the immediate run and the long-term village.

## Core Loop

```
[Diagram of the fundamental play loop]

Example:
Roll Dice → Move on Board → Trigger Event → Resolve Event →
Return to Center → Choose: Continue Up or Loop Around →
(Loop) Next Roll
```

## Unique Selling Proposition

[What makes this game different from every other game in the genre?]

> Unlike other deckbuilder roguelikes, Circle of Faith uses dice-driven circular board movement where equipment weight is a strategic resource, and a persistent village creates long-term progression between runs.

## Comparable Titles

| Game | Similarity | Differentiation |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| Slay the Spire | Deckbuilding combat | We use dice movement + village meta |
| Dicey Dungeons | Dice as core mechanic | We have circular board + equipment weight |
| Stardew Valley | Village building | Our village is a meta-progression hub |
